ucs.title: [LLM] Ferocious Kitten (G0137) RTLO masquerade — U+202E in dropped filenames
ucs.description: Hunts for files dropped to disk whose name contains the Right-to-Left Override character (U+202E). This is a hallmark of Ferocious Kitten / MarkiRAT lures (per Kaspersky, June 2021), which abuse RTLO to render an executable as 'document_fdp.exe' visually flipping to 'document_exe.pdf' so Persian-speaking targets (activists, dissidents inside Iran) double-click what looks like a benign PDF/JPG/DOC.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action IN ("created","modified","renamed") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.process_guid 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` 
| eval rtlo_in_name=if(match(file_name, "\x{202e}"), 1, 0) 
| eval rtlo_in_path=if(match(file_path, "\x{202e}"), 1, 0) 
| where rtlo_in_name=1 OR rtlo_in_path=1 
| eval is_executable=if(match(lower(file_name), "\.(exe|scr|com|cpl|hta|js|vbs|lnk|bat|jar)$"), 1, 0) 
| where is_executable=1 
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` 
| table firstTime, lastTime, dest, user, file_name, file_path, process_name, count
ucs.kql:
// Ferocious Kitten / MarkiRAT — RTLO (U+202E) filename spoofing
let RTLO = "\u202E";
let ExecExt = dynamic([".exe",".scr",".com",".cpl",".hta",".js",".vbs",".lnk",".bat",".jar",".ps1"]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| where FileName contains RTLO or FolderPath contains RTLO or PreviousFileName contains RTLO
// Filter to actually-executable real extensions (RTLO hides the real ext from the user, not from the FS)
| extend RealExt = tolower(strcat(".", tostring(split(FileName, ".")[-1])))
| where RealExt in (ExecExt)
// Drop Persian / Arabic / Hebrew dev or localisation paths that legitimately use RTL marks
| where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\" and FolderPath !has @"\Microsoft\\Edge\\"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          FileName, FolderPath, RealExt, SHA256,
          Dropper = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          DropperCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          DropperPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Ferocious Kitten / MarkiRAT — RTLO (U+202E) filename spoofing (cross-vendor via ASIM)
let RTLO = "\u202E";
let ExecExt = dynamic([".exe",".scr",".com",".cpl",".hta",".js",".vbs",".lnk",".bat",".jar",".ps1"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| where TargetFileName contains RTLO or TargetFilePath contains RTLO
| extend RealExt = tolower(strcat(".", tostring(split(TargetFileName, ".")[-1])))
| where RealExt in (ExecExt)
| where TargetFilePath !startswith @"C:\Windows\"
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          TargetFileName, TargetFilePath, RealExt, TargetFileSHA256,
          Dropper = ActingProcessName, DropperCmd = ActingProcessCommandLine
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Ferocious Kitten (G0137) RTLO Filename Masquerade
id: 8d6d3c2f-b1a4-4f8b-9b8a-fk0137rtlo01
status: experimental
description: Detects files written to disk whose name contains a Right-to-Left Override character (U+202E), masking the true extension. Tradecraft signature of Ferocious Kitten / MarkiRAT (Kaspersky 2021) used against Persian-speaking targets in Iran.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0137/
  - https://securelist.com/ferocious-kitten-6-years-of-covert-surveillance-in-iran/102806/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains: "\u202e"
  filter_localised:
    TargetFilename|startswith:
      - 'C:\Windows\'
      - 'C:\Program Files\Microsoft\\Edge\\'
  condition: selection and not filter_localised
fields:
  - Image
  - TargetFilename
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Rare RTL-language application installers; whitelist by signed-publisher hash
level: high
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1036.002
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.g0137
ucs.rationale: RTLO in filenames is rare in benign enterprise telemetry — the Unicode bidi-control character has essentially no legitimate use in executable filenames on Windows. Kaspersky's June 2021 report specifically called out RTLO-flipped lures (e.g. legitimate-looking PDF/JPG names with hidden .exe) as a Ferocious Kitten signature, distinguishing it from broader Iranian clusters (APT33, Charming Kitten, M
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Ferocious Kitten — MarkiRAT Telegram Desktop / Chrome shortcut hijack
ucs.description: Hunts the persistence pattern Kaspersky tied to MarkiRAT: a binary masquerading as 'Updater.exe' / 'chrome_update.exe' written into the user-writable Telegram Desktop folder, or a Chrome/Telegram .lnk shortcut whose target is rewritten to launch the implant before the legitimate app. Telegram and Chrome are the two most-used apps by Persian-speaking targets bypassing Iranian censorship — making them the natural hijack surface for this actor.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action IN ("created","modified","renamed") (Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\Telegram Desktop\\*" OR Filesystem.file_name IN ("chrome_update.exe","Updater.exe") OR Filesystem.file_name="*Telegram*.lnk" OR Filesystem.file_name="*chrome*.lnk") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.process_path 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` 
| eval suspicious_writer=if(NOT match(lower(process_name), "^(telegram\.exe|updater\.exe|msiexec\.exe|explorer\.exe|googleupdate\.exe|chrome\.exe|setup\.exe)$"), 1, 0) 
| where suspicious_writer=1 
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` 
| table firstTime, lastTime, dest, user, file_name, file_path, process_name, process_path, count
ucs.rationale: Ferocious Kitten's MarkiRAT specifically piggy-backs on Telegram Desktop and Chrome — both are the censorship-evasion staples of the actor's victim pool (Persian-speaking dissidents inside Iran). Generic 'AppData persistence' detections fire on hundreds of legit installers, but pinning to (a) writes into %APPDATA%\Telegram Desktop\ by a process other than Telegram/Updater itself, (b) re-targeted C
